notes: Two-repo effort. Phase 1 tasks land in the separate `dork-labs/blintz` repo (local: /Users/doriancollier/Keep/144/blintz) and are a hard prerequisite for Phase 2. Phase 2 tasks land in the DorkOS worktree ~/.dork/workspaces/dorkos/canvas-blintz-editor (branch canvas-blintz-editor). The Blintz <-> DorkOS bridge during development is `yalc` (npx yalc), not an npm publish. Untracked in Linear (no tracker projection).

tasks.subject: [canvas-markdown-editing] [P1] Add editable prop + master switch + block-edit gating to Blintz
tasks.description:
In /Users/doriancollier/Keep/144/blintz/packages/blintz: add a first-class read-only mode to the editor.

1. MarkdownEditor.tsx — add `editable?: boolean` to MarkdownEditorProps with TSDoc: 'Whether the document can be edited. Defaults to true. When false, the editor renders read-only: ProseMirror editing is disabled and the editing chrome (slash menu, block handle, selection toolbar, drag handles, link-edit popovers, placeholder) is suppressed.' Thread `editable` through MarkdownEditor -> EditorInner -> useBlintzEditor (add to EditorInnerProps and the useBlintzEditor call).

2. useBlintzEditor.ts — accept `editable` (default true). Capture once in a ref (`const editableRef = useRef(editable)`) to match the v1 'read at construction' model (consistent with seedRef/placeholderRef). Import `editorViewOptionsCtx` from '@milkdown/kit/core'. In the first `.config((ctx) => { ... })` block, after the existing ctx.set calls, add: `ctx.update(editorViewOptionsCtx, (prev) => ({ ...prev, editable: () => editableRef.current }))`. This is the master switch: ProseMirror disables direct editing and `view.editable` becomes false, which the toolbar, placeholder, table, list-item, image-block, code-block, and latex features ALREADY honor (verified — they each read `view.editable`).

3. Gate block-edit (the ONLY feature with no editable guard). Wrap its registration: replace the unconditional `blockEditFeature(editor, pluginViewFactory);` with `if (editableRef.current) { blockEditFeature(editor, pluginViewFactory); }`. Add a comment explaining: block-edit (slash menu + '+/::' block handle + drag) is pure editing chrome with no runtime editable guard of its own, and the block handle shows on hover via BlockProvider's own listeners; gating registration is the clean v1 approach (the prop is captured at construction and DorkOS remounts on toggle). Reactive editable (toggling without remount) is recorded as future Blintz polish.

Leave all other feature registrations unconditional — they self-gate on view.editable and several (code, image, table, list) are needed to RENDER content in read-only.

Acceptance: editable defaults to true (existing consumers unaffected); when editable={false}, no slash menu/block handle, no toolbar, no placeholder, contentEditable off; content (headings, lists, code with highlighting, tables, images, links, math) still renders.
tasks.activeForm: Adding editable prop and chrome gating to Blintz

tasks.subject: [canvas-markdown-editing] [P1] Test the Blintz read-only logic (node-env)
tasks.description:
Blintz tests are node-env vitest (vitest.config.ts: environment 'node', include packages/*/src/**/*.test.ts) with NO jsdom/testing-library installed, so a full editor render test is out of scope. Add an infra-appropriate unit test that locks the read-only contract at the seam we control.

Create packages/blintz/src/features/block-edit/gating.test.ts (or extend an existing pure test) that asserts the gating predicate: a small exported helper or a direct test that `editable` defaulting and the `if (editableRef.current)` registration decision behave correctly. Since registration happens inside the React `useEditor` factory (not unit-testable in node), instead extract NOTHING that over-engineers; prefer a focused test of any pure helper introduced. If no pure helper is warranted, add a type-level/contract assertion test that `MarkdownEditorProps` includes an optional `editable?: boolean` (compile-time) and document in the test file why a full render test is deferred (needs a jsdom + ProseMirror harness, tracked as follow-up).

Do not add jsdom/testing-library to Blintz just for this — that is a larger infra change. Keep the existing `npm test` (vitest run) green.

Acceptance: `npm test` in the Blintz repo passes; the new test documents and guards the editable contract at the level the current infra supports.

tasks.subject: [canvas-markdown-editing] [P1] Bump Blintz to 0.2.0, document editable, build + yalc publish
tasks.description:
1. Bump packages/blintz/package.json version 0.1.0 -> 0.2.0 (new minor: additive `editable` prop).
2. Document the `editable` prop in the Blintz README (packages/blintz/README.md or repo README): a short 'Read-only mode' section showing `<MarkdownEditor value={md} editable={false} />` and noting it suppresses all editing chrome.
3. Build: `npm run build -w blintz` (vite build) — must emit dist with the new prop in the .d.ts.
4. Make the local build available to DorkOS via yalc: from packages/blintz run `npx yalc publish` (copies the built package into the local yalc store). This is the co-dev bridge; do NOT `npm publish` (that is the human-gated release step).

Acceptance: blintz builds clean; dist/index.d.ts shows `editable?: boolean` on MarkdownEditorProps; `npx yalc publish` reports the package pushed to the store at 0.2.0.

tasks.subject: [canvas-markdown-editing] [P2] yalc-link Blintz 0.2.0 into the DorkOS worktree
tasks.description:
In the worktree ~/.dork/workspaces/dorkos/canvas-blintz-editor: overlay the local Blintz 0.2.0 build (from task 1.3) so the `editable` prop resolves at type-check and runtime.

Run `npx yalc add blintz` (or `npx yalc link blintz`) in apps/client of the worktree, then `pnpm install` at the worktree root. `.yalc/` and `yalc.lock` are gitignored co-dev artifacts. package.json keeps the npm spec (^0.1.0) per the spec; yalc overlays node_modules. Verify apps/client/node_modules/blintz/dist/index.d.ts contains `editable?: boolean`.

Acceptance: importing `{ MarkdownEditor }` from 'blintz' in the worktree resolves a build that types `editable`; `pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck` no longer errors on the editable prop usage (after task 2.1).
tasks.activeForm: yalc-linking Blintz into the DorkOS worktree

tasks.subject: [canvas-markdown-editing] [P2] Create BlintzCanvas.tsx lazy controlled wrapper
[canvas-markdown-editing] [P2] Add canvasEditing transient store flag
tasks.description:
apps/client/src/layers/shared/model/app-store/app-store-canvas.ts — add transient editing state to CanvasSlice (NOT persisted; live UI state for the active canvas only, must be absent from writeCanvasSession calls).

- Interface: add `canvasEditing: boolean;` and `setCanvasEditing: (editing: boolean) => void;` to CanvasSlice (with TSDoc).
- Creator: initialize `canvasEditing: false` and `setCanvasEditing: (editing) => set({ canvasEditing: editing })`. Do NOT write it through writeCanvasSession (loadCanvasForSession should also reset canvasEditing to false on session load to avoid a stale-editing carryover — add `canvasEditing: false` to both set() branches of loadCanvasForSession).

Also extend the dispatcher's structural store type in apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/ui-action-dispatcher.ts: add `canvasEditing: boolean;` to the DispatcherStore interface (the // Canvas group). The real callers pass useAppStore.getState() so the field flows automatically once on the slice.

Acceptance: `pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck` passes; canvasEditing is reset on session load and never serialized.

tasks.subject: [canvas-markdown-editing] [P2] Dispatcher 'protect the edit' guard
tasks.description:
apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/ui-action-dispatcher.ts — while the user is editing (`store.canvasEditing === true`), agent pushes must not overwrite the canvas markdown content.

- In `case 'open_canvas'`: guard the content application — `if (command.content != null && !store.canvasEditing) { store.setCanvasContent(command.content); }`. The panel-reveal side effects (setCanvasOpen/setRightPanelOpen/setActiveRightPanelTab) and preferredWidth STILL run; only the content replacement is skipped while editing.
- In `case 'update_canvas'`: `if (!store.canvasEditing) { store.setCanvasContent(command.content); }` — when editing, the agent push is ignored entirely (update_canvas has no other side effects).

Add a short comment referencing ADR-0292 (protect the edit). Note: only the markdown editor sets canvasEditing true, and non-markdown canvas content (url/json) is never edited, so this guard is effectively markdown-scoped in practice. The editor's own autosave calls setCanvasContent directly (not through the dispatcher), so it is unaffected.

Acceptance: with canvasEditing true, neither open_canvas nor update_canvas calls setCanvasContent; with it false, both do (as today).

tasks.subject: [canvas-markdown-editing] [P2] Rework CanvasMarkdownContent (toggle, draft, autosave, guards)
tasks.description:
apps/client/src/layers/features/canvas/ui/CanvasMarkdownContent.tsx — replace the streamdown renderer with the unified Blintz view/edit surface.

Props:
```
interface CanvasMarkdownContentProps {
  content: Extract<UiCanvasContent, { type: 'markdown' }>;
  /** Persist an edited markdown document back to canvas state. */
  onContentChange: (content: UiCanvasContent) => void;
}
```
State/refs: `isEditing` (useState false), `draft` (useState), `owningSessionRef` (useRef<string|null>), debounce `timerRef` (useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>|null>). Read `canvasSessionId` and `setCanvasEditing` via useAppStore selectors; use useAppStore.getState() inside callbacks to read the live canvasSessionId at fire time.

Lazy editor: `const BlintzCanvas = lazy(() => import('./BlintzCanvas').then((m) => ({ default: m.BlintzCanvas })))`. Render inside <Suspense fallback={<lightweight placeholder>}>. Key the editor by mode: `key={isEditing ? 'edit' : 'view'}` so editable is fixed at construction (matches Blintz v1 remount model).

View mode: `<BlintzCanvas value={content.content} editable={false} />` (value tracks live store content so agent updates refresh the view).
Edit mode: `<BlintzCanvas value={draft} editable onChange={handleChange} />`.

Enter edit: owningSessionRef.current = useAppStore.getState().canvasSessionId; setDraft(content.content); setIsEditing(true); setCanvasEditing(true).
Exit edit (toggle off): flush pending save (guarded), setIsEditing(false), setCanvasEditing(false).

handleChange(markdown): setDraft(markdown); clear+reschedule timer to ~500ms -> handlePersist(markdown); null the timer ref after it fires (so the unmount/exit flush does not double-write).
handlePersist(markdown): SESSION GUARD — `if (useAppStore.getState().canvasSessionId !== owningSessionRef.current) return;` then `onContentChange({ ...content, content: markdown })`.
Flush on unmount: useEffect cleanup — if timerRef pending, run the guarded persist with the latest draft, and call setCanvasEditing(false) if still editing.
Exit edit on session change: useEffect watching canvasSessionId — if it differs from owningSessionRef.current while editing, setIsEditing(false)+setCanvasEditing(false) (editor remounts fresh for the new session).

Toggle control: a single ghost icon Button (shared/ui) sticky top-right of the markdown pane (inside this component, NOT CanvasHeader). View shows Pencil (lucide-react) aria-label='Edit document'; edit shows Check aria-label='Finish editing'. focus-visible ring per components rule. Keep the prose wrapper container (`relative` so the sticky button anchors).

Remove the streamdown import and 'streamdown/styles.css'.

Acceptance: renders read-only by default; Edit seeds draft + sets canvasEditing; onChange debounce-persists once; flush-on-exit persists latest; session guard skips cross-session writes; Done returns to read-only. Typecheck + lint clean.

tasks.subject: [canvas-markdown-editing] [P2] Thread onContentChange through AgentCanvas
tasks.description:
apps/client/src/layers/features/canvas/ui/AgentCanvas.tsx — the markdown branch in CanvasBody now needs a persist callback. CanvasBody already receives `onSetContent: (content: UiCanvasContent | null) => void` (it is `setCanvasContent`). Pass it into the markdown branch as `onContentChange`:
`{canvasContent.type === 'markdown' && (<CanvasMarkdownContent content={canvasContent} onContentChange={onSetContent} />)}`.
No other change — both CanvasContent and AgentCanvas already thread setCanvasContent as onSetContent.

Acceptance: typecheck passes; markdown canvas persists edits through the existing per-session setCanvasContent.

tasks.subject: [canvas-markdown-editing] [P2] Tests: CanvasMarkdownContent, dispatcher guard, store flag; fix existing canvas tests
tasks.description:
Vitest + RTL + jsdom, co-located. 1. New apps/client/src/layers/features/canvas/__tests__/CanvasMarkdownContent.test.tsx: `vi.mock('../ui/BlintzCanvas', ...)` returning a stub that renders `value` in a data-testid and exposes a button that calls `onChange('edited')` (mirrors the streamdown mock pattern). Mock useAppStore for canvasSessionId + setCanvasEditing. Use fake timers for debounce. Cover: renders read-only by default (stub receives editable={false} + content.content); clicking Edit sets editable={true}, seeds draft, calls setCanvasEditing(true); onChange triggers ONE debounced onContentChange({...content, content:'edited'}) after 500ms (not per keystroke); flush-on-exit persists the latest draft; SESSION GUARD — when getState().canvasSessionId changes to a different value than captured at edit-start, the pending persist is skipped (no onContentChange) and edit mode exits; toggling Done returns to read-only. Each test carries a purpose comment. 2. Extend ui-action-dispatcher.test.ts: makeMockStore adds canvasEditing:false + setCanvasEditing: vi.fn(). Add cases: with canvasEditing:true, update_canvas does NOT call setCanvasContent and open_canvas does NOT call setCanvasContent but DOES still call setRightPanelOpen/setActiveRightPanelTab/setCanvasOpen; with canvasEditing:false, both still call setCanvasContent (existing behavior). 3. New (or extend store test) for app-store-canvas: setCanvasEditing flips the flag; it is not written to writeCanvasSession; loadCanvasForSession resets canvasEditing to false. 4. Fix existing canvas tests that now mount markdown content through the lazy BlintzCanvas: CanvasContent.test.tsx and AgentCanvas.test.tsx render markdown content ({type:'markdown'}). Add `vi.mock('../ui/BlintzCanvas', () => ({ BlintzCanvas: ({ value }) =>
{value}
}))` to both so jsdom never loads the real editor, and extend their mock store with canvasEditing/setCanvasEditing + canvasSessionId as needed. Keep their existing assertions green. Acceptance: `pnpm --filter @dorkos/client test -- --run` passes for the canvas + dispatcher suites; no real blintz load in jsdom.

tasks.subject: [canvas-markdown-editing] [P2] Docs: canvas view/edit model + blintz/yalc co-dev note
tasks.description:
Light docs to keep the codebase honest (AGENTS.md quality bar):
- Update the canvas feature module TSDoc (CanvasMarkdownContent / canvas barrel) to describe the view/edit model (one Blintz instance, read-only in view, editable in edit, autosave + protect-the-edit + session guard).
- Add a short note to a contributing guide (e.g. contributing/design-system.md or a canvas note) that the canvas markdown surface is now Blintz (not streamdown), consumed from npm with yalc as the local co-dev overlay for in-flight Blintz changes.
Keep it brief; no em-dashes.

Acceptance: docs build/lint unaffected; the view/edit model and blintz+yalc workflow are discoverable.

tasks.subject: [canvas-markdown-editing] [P3] Verify Blintz: typecheck + test + build
tasks.description: In /Users/doriancollier/Keep/144/blintz: run `npm run typecheck`, `npm test`, and `npm run build -w blintz`. All must pass. Confirm dist/index.d.ts exposes `editable?: boolean`. This gate must pass before the DorkOS verify trusts the yalc overlay.

tasks.subject: [canvas-markdown-editing] [P3] Verify DorkOS: typecheck + lint + tests + client build
tasks.description: In the worktree: `pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @dorkos/client lint`, `pnpm --filter @dorkos/client test -- --run` (at least the canvas + shared/lib suites), and `pnpm --filter @dorkos/client build`. All green. Watch for: the jsdoc-on-exports lint rule (BlintzCanvas/new exports), FSD layer imports (BlintzCanvas in features importing only blintz + nothing up-layer), prettier/tailwind class order. If the full client build chokes on the yalc-linked blintz ESM/CSS, capture the error and resolve (e.g. ensure blintz dist + styles.css resolve).

tasks.subject: [canvas-markdown-editing] [P3] (Best-effort) browser smoke of the editable canvas
tasks.description: If feasible unattended: run the worktree dev server, open /session, have an agent (or seed) a markdown canvas, verify it renders read-only via Blintz, click Edit (pencil), type, confirm autosave persists and Done returns to read-only, and that an agent update while editing is ignored (protect-the-edit). If browser automation is too flaky to run unattended, record this as a manual REVIEW-gate checklist item rather than blocking. Do not leave a dev server running.
